segments.s777-g1: چو + past verb is a temporal clause: چو بگذشت = 'when ... had passed'. سال 'year' is the subject of بگذشت. از برم is the phrase 'from/on me' (از + بر + ـم) meaning 'over me' or 'since my (birth)'. شست و پنج is the numeral 'sixty-five' (classical form). Word order is temporal clause (چو ...) preceding the main clause.
segments.s777-g2: فزون کردم is a compound where فزون (in the sense 'increasing/more') combines with کردم 'I did/made' to mean 'I increased' or 'I became more full of'. The following nouns اندیشه، درد، رنج are coordinated direct objects (or predicates of state) listing what increased. The verb کردم is first-person past.
segments.s777-g3: به + noun phrase marks the object/goal: به تاریخ شاهان means 'for/the history of the kings'. نیاز آمدم is an idiomatic perf. construction 'نیاز آمدم' = 'I came to need / I felt the need'. آمدم is first-person past 'I came' used here with نیاز to express 'I needed'.
segments.s777-g4: به پیش = 'to the presence of' (prepositional phrase). اختر دیرساز is a compound/name (اختر 'star' used as a name or epithet, دیرساز an epithet) acting as the person before whom the speaker came. آمدم again is 'I came' (past). The whole means 'I went to the presence of Akhtar Dirsaz.'
segments.s777-g5: This noun phrase lists the authors: بزرگان 'the great ones' (nobles/elders) و بادانش آزادگان is 'and the free (nobles) who are learned' — بادانش is the fused form of با + دانش meaning 'knowledgeable/learned', modifying آزادگان 'the free/noble ones'. The whole phrase is the subject of the verb in the next segment.
segments.s777-g6: نبشتند is the past plural verb 'they wrote' (here describing what the subjects did). یکسر 'entirely/altogether' and همه 'all' are adverbial/quantifying modifiers; رایگان 'free/of charge' states that the writing was given without payment. Typical Classical Persian order: subject ... verb ... modifiers.
segments.s777-g7: نشسته is a perfect-participial form meaning 'seated' or 'sitting' and functions as a circumstantial participle ('sitting, ...'). نظاره من 'my watching/observation' places the speaker as the experiencer (note possessive order: noun then possessor), and از دورشان 'from their distance' = 'from afar, watching them'. The whole segment describes the speaker's stance/position.
segments.s777-g8: تو گفتی is 'you said' (past second person); the clause is reported without an explicit complementizer, so بدم functions as the content of what was said — here 'بدم' = 'I was bad / I was at fault' or 'that I was wicked'. پیش مزدورشان is a locative/prepositional phrase 'in the presence of their mercenaries' (پیش = before/in the presence of; مزدورشان = their hired men).
segments.s777-g33: 'که' here introduces a wish-clause; the optative particle 'باد' (literally 'may') follows the adjective 'جاوید' so the phrase 'جاوید باد' means 'may he be everlasting'. 'آن خردمند مرد' is a demonstrative + noun phrase 'that wise man' (آن = 'that', خردمند = 'wise', مرد = 'man/person'). Note the archaic poetic ordering where the predicate adjective precedes the optative verb.
segments.s777-g34: همیشه is an adverb 'always' modifying the verbal phrase. 'به کام دلش' is a prepositional phrase 'to/for the desire of his heart' with the possessive enclitic -ش on دل; it expresses that actions matched his desires. 'کار کرد' is the simple past 'did/worked' and here idiomatically means 'things went/acted' — 'he made things happen' or 'it went his way.'
segments.s777-g35: همش ('all of it') introduces a list of attributes. The coordinated nouns 'رای و هم دانش و هم نسب' ('counsel and also learning and also lineage') use repeated هم for emphasis, listing qualities attributed to the person named earlier; the sequence is a nominal apposition without an explicit verb.
segments.s777-g36: This is a pair of epithets presented in nominal form: 'چراغِ عجم' (lamp/light of the Persians) and 'آفتابِ عرب' (sun of the Arabs). Classical Persian poetry often omits the ezāfe particle in such condensed appositions; both چراغ and آفتاب function as metaphoric predicates describing the subject.
segments.s777-g37: سرآمد here is a noun meaning 'conclusion' (the end of something). 'کنون' ('now') and 'قصه یزدگرد' ('the tale/story of Yazdegerd') form a temporal-stative clause meaning 'now the story of Yazdegerd has ended.' The verb 'is' or 'has come' is implied in the classical poetic statement.
segments.s777-g38: 'به' introduces a temporal specification 'in/at (the time of)'. 'ماه' here = 'month', 'سفندارمذ' is the proper month-name (Spandarmad), and 'روز ارد' is the named day (the day Ord). The whole phrase gives the dating: in the month Spandarmad, on the day Ord; note classical word order and the use of proper names for month/day.
segments.s777-g39: 'ز' is the poetic preposition 'from/since', introducing a temporal span measured from the Hijra ('هجرت'). 'شده' is the past participle/auxiliary 'has become/has passed', and the numeric phrase 'پنج هشتاد' is the classical ordering for 580 (five hundred eighty). 'بار' here means 'times/occasions' and marks the counted years/times that have passed.
segments.s777-g40: 'به نام' is the customary formula 'in the name of'. 'جهانداور کردگار' is a compound divine epithet: جهانداور ('world-judge' or 'arbiter of the world') with کردگار ('the Creator/God') as an attributive/title; the sequence invokes God by these names as part of the dating formula.
segments.s777-g9: جز introduces an exception ('except'). احسنت here is treated as a quoted/externalized word or short praise ('“well done”') functioning as the only thing received. از ایشان ('from them') marks the source. نبد is an archaic negative-past copula meaning 'was not / did not exist' (classical contraction), and بهره‌ام is 'my share/portion' with the first-person enclitic -م attached. The order is exception → item → source → negative verb → possessed noun (poetic inversion).
segments.s777-g10: بکفت is an archaic past/predicative meaning 'was enough / sufficed'. اندر is the locative/prepositional 'in/within'. احسنتشان is the same noun 'ahsan(t)' with the third-person plural possessive suffix '-شان' ('their “well done”'). زهره‌ام is a possessed noun ('my Zohreh' — here a proper name or epithet) with the -م suffix. Word order puts the predicate 'was enough' first, then the locative phrase, then the thing that sufficed (poetic topicalization).
segments.s777-g11: سر here means 'head' (literal/part) and takes the genitive construction 'سرِ بدره‌های کهن' ('the head(s) of the old/ancient بدرهها'). بدره‌های کهن = 'ancient full-moons / ancient beauties' (a poetic epithet), with کهن placed after the noun as a postpositive adjective. بسته شد is a passive/resultative phrase 'was bound/was closed' using شد as the past auxiliary. The line uses poetic inversion but follows a classical past description.
segments.s777-g12: وزان = 'and from that / and because of that' (compound prepositional/temporal adverb). بند روشن here uses بند in the poetic sense 'stanza/verse' (a unit of poetry), modified by روشن 'bright/clear'. دلم is the possessive construction 'دل + -م' = 'my heart' (the annotator split the -م separately; here it is the first-person possessive suffix). خسته شد = 'became tired / grew weary' (شد as past auxiliary). The clause reads as cause/result: from that bright stanza my heart became weary.
segments.s777-g13: ازین is the prepositional 'from these' (از + این; the annotator split it into از + ین — ین here is the demonstrative 'this/these'). نامور نامداران is a stacked epithet: نامور 'renowned' modifying نامداران 'the renowned/illustrious ones' (classical poetic pleonasm). شهر 'of the city' specifies which group; the whole phrase is a partitive/source phrase ('from among these famed, illustrious men of the city').
segments.s777-g14: علی دیلمی بود is straightforward: proper name + nisbah 'Daylamite' followed by بود, the past copula 'was'. کو is an archaic/poetic relative pronoun ('who/which') linking to the following descriptor (classical usage instead of که). راست بهر is a compact predicate: راست 'true/upright' and بهر 'for/toward' (a postpositional sense 'in respect of / for the purpose of'); together they describe his character or allegiance. Word order: subject — copula — relative clause/qualifier.
segments.s777-g15: که is the relative pronoun 'who/that' (linking back to the person). همواره 'always' is an adverb modifying کارش 'his work/affairs' (کار + possessive -ش). بخوبی 'well' is an adverb of manner. روان here is adjectival/predicative 'flowing/fluently running' describing کارش; Persian often omits an explicit copula in such predicates, so the sense is 'his work was/ran smoothly.'
segments.s777-g16: به نزد is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in the presence of / before' (used to name the audience or circle). بزرگان is the plural noun 'the great/elder ones' and روشن‌روان is a compound epithet 'bright‑soul / clear‑souled' placed after بزرگان as a descriptive epithet. The whole phrase localizes the praise/evaluation to that notable circle.
segments.s777-g17: This is a nominal clause: proper name + epithet (حسین قتیب) is the subject, followed by the copula است. The prepositional phrase از آزادگان classifies him as ‘one of the آزادگان’ (literally “from the free”), i.e. a member of that group.
segments.s777-g18: The relative clause is introduced by که (who/that). از من sets the source/recipient ('from me' / 'of me'), followed by the negative present form نخواهد (lit. ‘does not want’ / ‘will not desire’). سخن رایگان is the direct object phrase ‘free speech/words’; together it means he does not want/accept free words from me. The verb is a simple present/near-future negative used here to express refusal.
segments.s777-g19: ازویم is a fused prepositional phrase 'از او + یم' meaning 'from him (to me)'. It is followed by a coordinated list of goods (خور و پوشش و سیم و زر). The conjunction و simply links the nouns in a catalogue of items received.
segments.s777-g20: وزو here functions as another prepositional element 'و از او' (and from him) before the verb یافتم (I found/obtained) in the past tense. The nouns جنبش و پای و پر form a coordinated list of things found/observed ('movement, foot, and feather'); the verb is transitive and takes that list as its object. (Note: some lexical items appear in archaic or contracted shape in the manuscript.)
segments.s777-g21: نیم آگه is a compound meaning 'half-aware' or 'only partially aware' (نیم modifying آگه). از introduces the domain of that awareness: اصل و فرع خراج ('the principal and the subsidiary/branch of the kharaj' — i.e. main and secondary components of the tax). Word order: adverbial/degree + adjective + prepositional phrase.
segments.s777-g22: همی‌غلتم is a present/habitual first-person verb 'I err / I keep erring' (همی- marks ongoing/habitual aspect). اندر میان دواج is a prepositional phrase 'in the midst between the two/pair' (دواج = pair/two), so the whole means 'I keep erring between the two.'
segments.s777-g23: This is a conditional clause introduced by اگر (if). جهاندار is the noun 'sovereign/ruler' and نیستی is the second-person negative/copula 'you are not'. تنگ‌دست follows as a descriptive adjective 'poor' or 'needy'; the clause reads 'if you are not a sovereign, (but) [are] poor'.
segments.s777-g24: مرا ... را is the object (me) with the object marker را separated in the manuscript. بر سر گاه is a locative phrase 'on/upon the head/top of the place' (سر = top/head, گاه = place/time). بودی نشست is a counterfactual/conditional past construction: بودی (you would have / 'you were' in a conditional sense) with نشست (to seat / to make sit), so the sense is 'you would have seated me at the head/foremost place.'
segments.s777-g41: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. این نامور‌نامه is the noun phrase 'this book of renown/epic' as subject. آمد is the simple past 'came/was brought into being'. به بن is a poetic locution: به + بن ('to the root/foundation') here idiomatically means 'came into being / was established'.
segments.s777-g42: ز من = 'because of / through me' (ز used causally). روی here means 'side/aspect' (from my side), qualifying the cause; کشور is the subject of the following verb (the realm), شود is the present/passive-like 'becomes', and پر سخن is a compound predicate 'full of speech' (پر = full (of) + سخن = speech/talk). The clause as a whole: 'because of me ... the realm became full of talk.'
segments.s777-g43: از آن پس is an adverbial phrase 'after that'. نمیرم is the negative present/optative of 'to die' (ن- prefix + میر/مر stem) meaning 'I do not/will not die'. که introduces an explanatory clause ('for/because'), and من زنده‌ام is the explanatory clause 'I am alive' with the copular suffix -ام marking 1st person.
segments.s777-g44: که again introduces explanation 'for/because'. تخم سخن من is a genitive phrase 'the seed of my speech/poetry' (تخم + سخن + possessor من). پراگنده‌ام is a predicate adjective 'is scattered / is widespread' with the 1st-person suffix -ام; in poetry the copula is attached to the adjective and refers back to the noun phrase.
segments.s777-g45: هر آنکس که دارد ... is a relative clause 'whoever has ...'. دارد is the present 'has/possesses'. The three nouns هش، رای، دین are coordinated by و: 'hush / wisdom (هش), counsel (رای), and religion/faith (دین)'; they form the qualities of the person referred to by هر آنکس.
segments.s777-g46: پس از مرگ is the temporal phrase 'after (my) death' (پس + از + مرگ). بر من is the prepositional object 'upon me / to me'. کند is the 3rd-person present tense 'he does/makes' used here to mean 'he will utter/do', and آفرین is an exclamation/noun 'praise' (i.e. 'say "afarin"/praise'). The whole clause means 'after death he will acclaim/praise me.'
segments.s777-g25: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause ('when'); سال is the subject 'year'; اندر آمد is a classical word order where the verb آمد ('came/arrived') follows the noun and the preposition اندر marks 'into/within'. به هفتاد و یک indicates the target year (literally 'into seventy and one') with به + number meaning 'into/reaching (the) seventy‑first'.
segments.s777-g26: همی is an emphatic/continuative adverb ('again/ever'); زیر بیت اندر is a prepositional location phrase ('beneath the couplet') placed before the verb for poetic word order; آرم is a 1st‑person poetic present/subjunctive form 'I place / may I place / I make to rest' and فلک is the object meaning 'the heavens' (literal 'sky') here; together the clause says the speaker places the heavens under his verse.
segments.s777-g27: همی here is an adverbial intensifier ('ever/always'); گاه means 'time/era'; محمود آباد is a predicative phrase 'Mahmud (be) prosperous' (آباد = 'prosperous'); باد is the classical optative particle 'may (it be)'. The word order places the time/subject before the optative copula.
segments.s777-g28: This is a pair of optative wishes. سرش = سر + enclitic ش ('his head'); سبز is the adjective 'green' (an idiom wishing long life); باد is the optative 'may it be'. و links the two wishes; دلش = 'his heart' and شاد = 'joyful'. The possessive enclitic ش attaches directly to the noun.
segments.s777-g29: چنانش is چنان + enclitic ش = 'thus/him' (i.e. 'him thus'); ستایم is a 1st‑person present/subjunctive or intentional form 'I will praise / I praise' and is followed by the conjunction که introducing a result/purpose clause ('so that'); اندر جهان 'in the world' gives the scope. Note the object enclitic is attached to چنان, and the verb follows it in classical order.
segments.s777-g30: سخن باشد is an optative/existential construction 'may there be talk' (باشد here functions as a subjunctive/optative 3rd person); از introduces the topic 'about/concerning'; آشکار و نهان are coordinated adjectives 'the manifest (open) and the hidden' meaning 'both openly and secretly'. The whole clause expresses the intended effect of the praise.
segments.s777-g31: مرا از بزرگان ستایش بود is a topical/existential clause: 'مرا' (me, here written as م + را in the list) is the patient/topic 'of me/for me', از بزرگان 'from the great ones' qualifies the source of the praise, and ستایش بود literally 'praise was' i.e. 'there was praise' (existential). The fronting of مرا gives emphasis—'I was praised by the great.'
segments.s777-g32: ستایش ورا = 'praise for him' (ورا is the older form of 'او را' = 'to/for him'); در فزایش = 'in increase' (an idiom meaning 'on the rise' or 'growing'); بود is the copula/existential 'was' indicating the state. Together: 'praise for him was increasing.'

sentences.s777-s1: When sixty-five years had passed over me, I increased my thought, pain, and suffering.
sentences.s777-s2: I found myself in need of the history of the kings; I came into the presence of the astrologer Akhtar Dirsaz.
sentences.s777-s3: Great men and learned freemen wrote everything outright, all without charge.
sentences.s777-s4: Seated, I observed them from afar; you said I was wicked before their mercenaries.
sentences.s777-s17: May that wise man be eternal; he always made things go as his heart desired.
sentences.s777-s18: All his counsel, his learning, and his lineage — a lamp of Persia, a sun of the Arabs.
sentences.s777-s19: Now the tale of Yazdegerd comes to its end, in the month Spandarmad, on the day Ord.
sentences.s777-s20: Since the Hijra five hundred eighty years have passed — in the name of the Creator, the world's-judge.
sentences.s777-s5: My only share from them was the word “Ah-sant”; in their “Ah-sant” my Zohreh sufficed.
sentences.s777-s6: The heads of the ancient beauties were bound, and from that bright stanza my heart became weary.
sentences.s777-s7: From these famed, illustrious men of the city was Ali the Daylamite, who was upright in purpose.
sentences.s777-s8: who always had his affairs flowing well before the great, clear‑souled elders.
sentences.s777-s9: Husayn Qatib is one of the free/noblemen, who will not accept my words for nothing.
sentences.s777-s10: From him I received food and clothing and silver and gold; and from him I found movement and foot and feather.
sentences.s777-s11: I am only half-aware of the principal and the branch of the tribute/tax; I keep erring between the two.
sentences.s777-s12: If you are not a sovereign (and are poor), you would have seated me at the head of the place.
sentences.s777-s21: When this book of renown came into being through me, the realm became full of talk.
sentences.s777-s22: After that I shall not die, for I am alive; the seed of my speech is widespread.
sentences.s777-s23: Whoever has intelligence, counsel, and faith will, after my death, praise me.
sentences.s777-s13: When the year entered its seventy‑first, I again set the heavens beneath a couplet.
sentences.s777-s14: May Mahmoud's time ever be prosperous; may his head be green and his heart joyful.
sentences.s777-s15: I will praise him thus, so that throughout the world there may be talk of him, both openly and secretly.
sentences.s777-s16: I had praise among the great; praise of him was on the increase.
